steps: Resuspend previously grown bacterial cells (approximately 50 mg of bacterial fresh biomass) in 400 µL de TE 1X (10mM of Tris-HCl, 1mM of EDTA pH 8,0).
Add 1 µL of lysozyme (100mg/ml) shortly vórtex, and incubate for 1 h at 37 °C.
Add 67,5 µL of SDS 10 % (pre-heated at 65 °C) and 2,5 µL of Proteinase K (10 mg/mL) and shortly vórtex, and incubate at 65 °C for 10 min.

Add 100 µL of NaCl at 5M and 100 µL of a solution of Cetyltrimethylammonium Bromide (CTAB)/NaCl (0,03 M/0,07 M), both pre-heated at 65 °C, and vortex until observing a whitish color and incubate at 65 °C for 10 min.
Add 750 µL of chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1), vortex for 10 s and centrifugate at 12.000 rpm for 5 min.
Transfer the supernatant (approximately 800 µL) to a new tube and add 0,6 volumes of isopropyl alcohol to precipitate the DNA and gently invert the tube.
Store at -20 °C for 30-60 min in case precipitated DNA is not observed.
Centrifugate at 4 °C at 12000 rpm for 5 min.
Discard the supernatant and add 1 mL of ice-cold ethanol 70% (-20 °C).
Centrifugate at 12.000 rpm for 5 min and discard the supernatant.
Centrifugate at 12.000 rpm for 5 min and discard the supernatant.
Centrifugate at 12.000 rpm at 4 °C for 1 min to let the ethanol evaporate at room temperature.
Invert the tubes, place them on absorbent paper, and then use a speedvac at 36 °C for 30 min to let them completely dry.
Resuspend the pellet in a volume of 20 µL of MilliQ water (add more water if necessary, according to the concentration of the pellet).
Check the DNA quality by migrating different concentrations of it and using a control λ phage concentration of 50 ng/µL in an agarose gel with 1 % of TBE 1X ran for 30 min at 5 V per cm 2
creator: Cristian Daniel Asmat Ortega
